title: BG11_and_inducer_preparation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a0dr1","text":"Protocol for the preparation of media and inducers used during the interlaboratory study published by Mager et al. 2023.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: %s %s Buffer solutiong/LHEPES, NaOH (pH 8)240
%s %s Stock 1g/LNaNO3600CaCl2 * 2H2O14.4
%s %s Stock 2g/LMgSO4 * 7H2O30FeCl3 * 6H2O1.62Na2EDTA * 2 H2O2.24Micronutrients400 mL
%s %s Stock 3g/LKH2PO416Na2EDTA * 2 H2O5.2
%s %s Stock 4g/LNaHCO380

%s %s Micronutrientsg/LH3BO32.86MnCl2 * 4H2O1.81ZnSO4 * 7H2O0.222Na2MoO4 * 2H2O0.391CuSO4 * 5H2O0.079*Co(NO3)2 6H2O0.049 * Copper sulfate is not to be added for the interlab study as we need copper free media for PpetE induction
A B C 1 Rhamnose stock solution g/L mM 2 Rhamnose 164 1000 Mass concentration of Rhamnose inducer stock solution 1
A B C 1 CuSO4 stock solution g/L mM 2 CuS04*5H20 0.0025 0.1 Mass concentration of copper sulfate inducer stock solution
Dissolve 24g of HEPES in 40 ml of MilliQ water
Under stirring adjust pH to 8 using NaOH
Filter sterilize the solution
Dissolve 1.4g CaCl2*2H2O in 70 ml of MilliQ water
Add 60g of NaNO3 under vigorous stirring at 50°C until fully dissolved.
Cool solution to room temperature and add MilliQ water to 100 ml.

BG11 medium for interlab study (after van Alphen et al., 2018) %s %s MacronutrientsmMg/LNaNO317.651.5KH2PO40.2940.04MgSO4 * 7H2O0.3040.075CaCl2 * 2H2O0.2440.036FeCl3 * 6H2O0.0150.004Na2EDTA * 2 H2O0.050.0186NaHCO39.441HEPES51.2MicronutrientsµMmg/LH3BO346.252.86MnCl2 * 4H2O9.151.81ZnSO4 * 7H2O0.770.222Na2MoO4 * 2H2O1.620.391CuSO4 * 5H2O0.3160.079Co(NO3)2 6H2O0.1680.049 van Alphen P, Abedini Najafabadi H, Branco dos Santos F, Hellingwerf KJ. Increasing the Photoautotrophic Growth Rate of Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 by Identifying the Limitations of Its Cultivation. Biotechnol J. 2018 Aug;13(8):1700764. DOI: 10.1002/biot.201700764
%s %s ComponentmL/LBuffer solution5 mLStock 12.5 mLStock 22.5 mLStock 32.5 mLStock 410.5 mLFill with autoclaved water to 1L
Add MilliQ water to 100 ml
Filter sterilize the solution
Dissolve 0.224g EDTA Na2*2H2O in 40 mL MilliQ water followed by 0.162g FeCl3·6H2O and leave the solution stirring over night
Dissolve 3g MgSo4*7H2O in 15 ml MilliQ water and add it to the EDTA Na2*2H2O + FeCl3·6H2O solution
Add 40 ml of Micronutrient solution (See Section Micronutrient stock solution preparation) to this mix and fill to 100 ml using MilliQ water
Filter sterilize the solution.
Dissolve 1.6 g K2HPO4 in 40 ml of MilliQ water
Dissolve 0.52 g EDTA Na2*2H2O in the same solution
Filter sterilize the solution
Fill to 100 ml using MilliQ water
Dissolve 8 g NaHCO3 in 80 ml MilliQ water
Fill to 100 ml using MilliQ water
Filter sterilize the solution
Add trace metal salts in the order they are listed to 100 ml of MilliQ water and wait for complete dissolution between each addition
Filter sterilize the solution
Dissolve 8.2 g of rhamnose in 40 ml MilliQ water
Add MilliQ water to 50ml
Filter sterilize the solution
Dissolve 25 mg CuS04*5H20 in 8 ml MiliQ water
Add to 10 ml using MilliQ water
Dilute 10 µl of this solution in 9.99 ml MilliQ water
Filter sterilize the solution

BG-11 is split into four stock solutions such that incompatible (i.e. because of low solubility) compounds at high concentration are avoided. Additionally, a minimal amount of HEPES buffer is added to ensure the same starting pH of the cultures. The final medium is prepared by adding buffer and each stock solution to 500 ml autoclaved water measuring cylinder and filling it to a total of 1 liter.
